title: Replace Tailwind CDN with built CSS and add favicon
analysis:
The dashboard loads Tailwind CSS via cdn.tailwindcss.com (a dev-only CDN that prints console warnings in production) and has no favicon (causing 404 errors). Additionally, Alpine.js uses an imprecise version pin (@3.x.x) and Chart.js uses a major-only pin (@4/). The fix involves: (1) replacing the Tailwind CDN with a locally-built CSS file using the Tailwind v4 standalone CLI, (2) adding an SVG favicon, (3) pinning all CDN deps to exact semver versions. The existing custom styles in style.css (x-cloak hiding, htmx transition effects) must be merged into the Tailwind build input so there's a single CSS output. The dashboard is a pure Python/FastAPI project with no Node.js infrastructure, making the standalone CLI the ideal choice — no package.json or npm needed. The built tailwind.css will be committed so the app works without a build step; the Makefile provides regeneration when templates change.

Post-review update: Review found 3 blocking issues. Issues 1 (broken_static_reference in base.html) and 2 (broken_test in test_scaffold.py) were already resolved by step-6 (commit c547928) — the reviewer was likely reviewing the diff rather than the final state. Issue 3 (silent_failure_at_boundary in Makefile curl) is still present: curl is invoked with -sL but without --fail, meaning a failed download (404, 403, rate-limit) exits 0 and writes error HTML to bin/tailwindcss, which Make then considers up-to-date. Steps 9-10 added to fix this with TDD.

prerequisites.description: Create dashboard/Makefile with css and css-watch targets using the Tailwind v4 standalone CLI. The Makefile should download the standalone binary to a local bin/ if not present, then run it against input.css to produce tailwind.css. Include a clean target.

Create dashboard/src/dashboard/static/input.css — the Tailwind build input file. Use @import 'tailwindcss' and @source '../templates' (Tailwind v4 CSS-first config). Include all custom styles currently in style.css: [x-cloak] hide rule, .htmx-settling and .htmx-swapping transition rules.

Create dashboard/src/dashboard/static/favicon.svg — a simple dark-themed SVG favicon representing Dark Factory (e.g., a gear or 'DF' monogram on a gray-900 rounded rect background with blue-400 accent). Keep it minimal (<1KB).

Run Tailwind CLI to generate dashboard/src/dashboard/static/tailwind.css from input.css. Verify the output contains Tailwind utility classes used in templates (bg-gray-900, text-gray-100, animate-pulse, etc.) and the custom rules from input.css.

steps.description: Write failing tests in test_app.py: (1) TestFavicon.test_favicon_link_in_html — GET / HTML contains <link rel="icon" referencing /static/favicon.svg, (2) TestFavicon.test_favicon_svg_served — GET /static/favicon.svg returns 200 with image/svg+xml content type.
Add <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/static/favicon.svg"> to the <head> of base.html. The favicon.svg file was created in pre-3. Both tests from step-1 should now pass.

Write failing tests in test_app.py: (1) TestTailwindBuild.test_no_cdn_tailwind — GET / HTML does NOT contain 'cdn.tailwindcss.com', (2) TestTailwindBuild.test_local_tailwind_css_linked — GET / HTML contains href="/static/tailwind.css", (3) TestTailwindBuild.test_tailwind_css_served — GET /static/tailwind.css returns 200 with text/css content type, (4) TestTailwindBuild.test_tailwind_css_has_utilities — GET /static/tailwind.css body contains key utility classes (bg-gray-900, text-gray-100).

Update base.html: remove <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>, add <link rel="stylesheet" href="/static/tailwind.css">. The built tailwind.css from pre-4 is already in static/. All tests from step-3 should pass.

Write failing tests in test_app.py: (1) TestCustomStyles.test_tailwind_css_has_xcloak — GET /static/tailwind.css contains '[x-cloak]', (2) TestCustomStyles.test_tailwind_css_has_htmx_settling — contains '.htmx-settling', (3) TestCustomStyles.test_tailwind_css_has_htmx_swapping — contains '.htmx-swapping'. Also: (4) TestCustomStyles.test_no_separate_style_css_link — GET / HTML does NOT contain 'style.css'.

Remove the <link rel="stylesheet" href="/static/style.css"> line from base.html (custom styles are now in tailwind.css via input.css). Delete dashboard/src/dashboard/static/style.css. Update test_scaffold.py: change TestStaticFiles.test_static_css_served to check /static/tailwind.css instead of /static/style.css.

Write failing tests in test_app.py: TestCdnVersionPinning class — (1) test_alpinejs_exact_version — GET / HTML contains 'alpinejs@3.15.8' (not '@3.x.x'), (2) test_chartjs_exact_version — GET / HTML contains 'chart.js@4.5.1' (not '@4/'), (3) test_no_wildcard_versions — GET / HTML does NOT match pattern '@\d+\.x' or '@\d+/' in any script src.

Update base.html script tags: change alpinejs@3.x.x to alpinejs@3.15.8, change chart.js@4/dist to chart.js@4.5.1/dist. Verify htmx@2.0.4 and idiomorph@0.3.0 are already exact-pinned (no change needed).

Write failing test in test_scaffold.py: TestMakefileCurlSafety.test_curl_uses_fail_flag — read the Makefile content, find all lines containing 'curl', and assert each includes --fail or the -f short flag (e.g., -fsSL). This catches the silent_failure_at_boundary issue where a failed HTTP download (404/403/rate-limit) would exit 0 and write error HTML to bin/tailwindcss.

Fix Makefile curl invocation: change 'curl -sL' to 'curl -fsSL' on line 13 (the $(TAILWIND_BIN) target). Also add '|| (rm -f $(TAILWIND_BIN); exit 1)' to ensure a failed download never leaves a partial file that satisfies the Make target. The test from step-9 should now pass.

design_decisions.decision: Use Tailwind v4 standalone CLI instead of PostCSS/npm build
design_decisions.rationale: The dashboard is a pure Python project with no Node.js infrastructure (no package.json, no node_modules). The standalone CLI is a single binary that requires no Node.js runtime, keeping the build dependency minimal. A Makefile wraps the binary for ergonomics.

design_decisions.decision: Use Tailwind v4 CSS-first config instead of v3 tailwind.config.js
design_decisions.rationale: Tailwind v4 uses @import 'tailwindcss' and @source directives directly in the input CSS file, eliminating the need for a separate tailwind.config.js. This is simpler and keeps all CSS configuration in one place.

design_decisions.decision: Commit built tailwind.css rather than .gitignore it
design_decisions.rationale: The dashboard must work without a build step (e.g., after git clone + uv sync + run). Committing the built CSS ensures the app is deployable immediately. The Makefile is for regeneration when templates change during development.

design_decisions.decision: Merge style.css into Tailwind input rather than keeping two CSS files
design_decisions.rationale: The existing style.css has only 3 rules (x-cloak, htmx-settling, htmx-swapping). Merging into input.css produces a single built CSS output, reducing HTTP requests and simplifying the template. The custom rules are appended after @import 'tailwindcss' in input.css.

design_decisions.decision: Pin CDN deps to exact versions rather than vendoring
design_decisions.rationale: Vendoring htmx/Alpine/Chart.js/idiomorph would add ~500KB of JS to the repo and require manual updates. Pinning to exact semver versions (e.g., @3.15.8 not @3.x.x) on jsdelivr/unpkg CDNs provides reproducibility with near-zero maintenance. Can vendor later if offline support is needed.

design_decisions.decision: Use SVG favicon instead of ICO/PNG
design_decisions.rationale: SVG favicons are vector (sharp at any size), tiny (<1KB), and supported by all modern browsers. An ICO would require multiple raster sizes and a larger file. The dashboard is an internal tool so legacy browser support is not a concern.

design_decisions.decision: Use curl --fail plus cleanup guard in Makefile download target
design_decisions.rationale: Without --fail, curl exits 0 on HTTP errors (404, 403, rate-limit) and writes the error-page HTML to the binary path. Make considers the target up-to-date, so subsequent invocations skip the download. The --fail flag makes curl exit non-zero on HTTP errors, and the '|| rm -f' guard ensures no partial file satisfies the Make target on failure.

reuse.how: Use the existing `client` fixture for all new HTTP-level tests (favicon serving, CSS serving, HTML content checks). All existing test classes use this pattern.
Follow the same pattern of testing static file serving (GET /static/X returns 200 with correct content-type). Update the existing test to check tailwind.css instead of style.css.

reuse.what: HTML content assertion pattern from TestIndex/TestHtmxErrorHandling
reuse.how: Follow the established pattern of `html = client.get('/').text` followed by `assert 'expected' in html` for checking base.html content (favicon link, CSS link, version strings).

reuse.what: Existing custom CSS rules from style.css
reuse.how: Copy the 3 custom rule blocks ([x-cloak], .htmx-settling, .htmx-swapping) into input.css after the @import directive, preserving exact property values.
